title: Blend Colour Palettes
text: objective blends blend colour palettes using blend modes, multiply screen.

title: Blend Colour Palettes
text: Install CRAN, development version GitHub.

title: Blend Colour Palettes
code: library(dplyr) library(jumble)  scales::show_col(c(teal, orange, blends::multiply(teal, orange)), ncol = 3)

title: blends: Blend Colour Palettes — blends-package
text: Blends colour palettes using digital blend modes multiply, screen, overlay. methods composite colours palette functions using Porter-Duff alpha compositing data visualisation.

title: blends: Blend Colour Palettes — blends-package
text: Maintainer: David Hodge davidhodge931@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Blend colours and palettes using colour burn mode — colour_burn
title: Blend colours and palettes using colour burn mode — colour_burn
text: Darkens destination colour reflect source increasing contrast. Produces deep, saturated results.

title: Blend colours and palettes using colour burn mode — colour_burn
Blend colours and palettes using colour burn mode — colour_burn
text: ... Either one two colour/palette arguments: one argument: colour palette blended two arguments: first blended second argument can character vector colours scales::pal_*() function

what: Blend colours and palettes using multiply mode — multiply

text: Darkens colours multiplying together. Creates darker, saturated results. Useful creating shadows, darkening backgrounds, adding depth.

what: Blend colours and palettes using overlay mode — overlay
title: Blend colours and palettes using overlay mode — overlay
text: Combines multiply screen depending lightness second colour. Values 50% grey multiplied (darkened); values screened (lightened).
